mechanism_narrative: A persistent downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the trailing three months signals monetary easing that lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, systematically producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month trend and 20-day forward SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or the sign of the correlation flips in more than one of the last three tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points from its trailing 12-month minimum (a Sahm-rule-style trigger), it signals an incipient labor market deterioration that historically precedes recessions, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as risk premia rise.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8% near multi-decade low as of 2018-06
failure_condition: In more than 40% of historical trigger events, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive, or the trigger fails to fire at least once in any documented pre-recession window since 1970 in backtest data.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth (RSAFS) combined with a still-low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) jointly signal robust consumer demand unconstrained by tight monetary policy, an interaction that predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; neither series alone captures this synergy since strong RSAFS growth under tightening FEDFUNDS instead compresses valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=501971 million and FEDFUNDS=1.7% as of snapshot
regime_hint: early-to-mid monetary tightening cycle with accommodative real rates
failure_condition: The joint-condition subset shows no statistically significant outperformance versus the unconditional 20-day SPX return distribution (t-test p > 0.10), or single-series RSAFS or FEDFUNDS models achieve equal or higher Rank IC than the interaction model.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and corporate earnings momentum, producing a short-lived positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days as markets incrementally price in improved production data.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX return autocorrelation with INDPRO month-over-month change is not significantly different from zero (p > 0.05) over rolling 3-year windows, or effect reverses sign in two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime characterized by unemployment near cyclical lows, decelerating PAYEMS growth (month-over-month net job additions falling below the trailing 6-month average) signals labor market exhaustion that precedes equity market weakness, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days; in early or mid-cycle regimes the same deceleration is noise.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM change falls below trailing 6-month average AND UNRATE < 4%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=3.8%, PAYEMS=148662 thousand as of snapshot
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with unemployment near multi-decade lows
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following the trigger in late-cycle regimes are not significantly more negative than baseline (p > 0.05), or the effect is equally strong in early-cycle regime subsamples, invalidating the regime-conditionality claim.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST (housing starts) of more than 10% from its trailing 6-month peak signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and forward-looking consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising mortgage rates
failure_condition: Triggered instances show positive median 20-day forward SPX returns, or Rank IC is below 0.03 across two consecutive tightening cycles in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the real policy rate, approximated by the gap between FEDFUNDS and trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation, turns positive and rising, tightening real financial conditions progressively compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; this effect depends on the joint level of both series rather than either alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation rate turns positive and increases for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=250.535 and FEDFUNDS=1.7% as of snapshot
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising real rates
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns during rising positive real-rate episodes are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) versus baseline, or the single-series FEDFUNDS model matches the interaction model's Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of historically low unemployment (UNRATE below 4%), continued acceleration in RSAFS month-over-month growth signals sustained consumer strength that supports near-term equity momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days; in higher-unemployment regimes the same RSAFS acceleration lacks predictive power because it may reflect precautionary rather than confident spending.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=3.8%, RSAFS=501971 million as of snapshot
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns conditional on the low-unemployment regime show no significant difference from the unconditional distribution (p > 0.10), or the same RSAFS signal performs equally well in high-unemployment subsamples.
